task.name: Bilateral Bargaining Protocol Expansion
task.description: Extend the platform's institutional visibility by adding TIOLI and Asymmetric Nash bargaining protocols, enabling study of how different sources of bargaining power (commitment vs institutional power vs patience) affect exchange outcomes.

vision_extraction.primary_goal: Make bargaining institutions visible and comparable by adding protocols with different power sources
success_criteria: New protocols have formal theoretical justification from O&R Bargaining
Protocols are swappable modules conforming to BargainingProtocol ABC
Theory tests validate protocol behavior against textbook predictions
UI clearly communicates protocol differences via labels and tooltips
deliverables: UI protocol selector with improved labels/tooltips

context.gap_analysis_summary: Core asymmetric_nash_bargaining_solution() exists but no protocol wrapper using agent bargaining_power. TIOLI not implemented. UI has basic protocol toggle but no tooltips or power config.
canonical_sources: Osborne & Rubinstein, Bargaining and Markets, Chapter 2 (Axiomatic bargaining)
Osborne & Rubinstein, Bargaining and Markets, Chapter 3 (Strategic bargaining, TIOLI)

phases.name: Core Protocol Implementation
phases.description: Add bargaining_power attribute to Agent and implement TIOLI and Asymmetric Nash protocol classes
phases.exit_criteria: Both protocols instantiable, basic solve() works, bargaining_power attribute exists on Agent
phases.gate_verification: from microecon import TIOLIBargainingProtocol, AsymmetricNashBargainingProtocol; from microecon.agent import Agent; assert hasattr(Agent, '__dataclass_fields__') and 'bargaining_power' in Agent.__dataclass_fields__

phases.name: Theory Tests
phases.description: Implement theory tests verifying O&R Chapter 2-3 properties for both protocols
phases.exit_criteria: All theory tests pass, properties verified against textbook

phases.name: Server & UI Integration
phases.description: Add server routing for new protocols, update UI with improved labels, tooltips, and bargaining_power distribution config
phases.entry_criteria: Phase 1 complete, protocols exported from microecon
phases.exit_criteria: New protocols selectable in UI, tooltips display, bargaining_power configurable
phases.gate_verification: Manual: Start frontend, verify all 4 protocols appear with tooltips

phases.name: Documentation
phases.description: Update CLAUDE.md, STATUS.md, and ensure code docstrings clearly explain protocol differences
phases.exit_criteria: Documentation accurately describes all protocols and their differences
phases.gate_verification: Review CLAUDE.md and STATUS.md for protocol documentation

gate_checks.1→2: Protocols importable: from microecon import TIOLIBargainingProtocol, AsymmetricNashBargainingProtocol
gate_checks.2→4: Theory tests pass: uv run pytest tests/theory/test_tioli*.py tests/theory/test_asymmetric*.py
gate_checks.3→4: UI functional: manual verification of protocol selector

features.name: Add bargaining_power attribute to Agent
features.description: Add bargaining_power: float = 1.0 attribute to Agent dataclass, representing intrinsic institutional bargaining power (w_i). Update create_agent() factory to accept this parameter.

functional: Agent has bargaining_power attribute with default 1.0

features.name: Implement TIOLI closed-form solution function
features.description: Implement tioli_bargaining_solution() that computes the allocation where proposer extracts all surplus. Responder receives bundle giving exactly their disagreement utility. For Cobb-Douglas, this has closed form.

functional: Function takes prefs_1, endowment_1, prefs_2, endowment_2, proposer (1 or 2)
property: Allocation is Pareto efficient (on contract curve)
Allocation is feasible (sums to total endowment)
theory: Proposer extracts maximum surplus consistent with responder IR constraint (O&R Ch 3)

features.name: Implement TIOLIBargainingProtocol class
features.description: Create TIOLIBargainingProtocol implementing BargainingProtocol ABC. Lower agent ID proposes by default. Document that proposer selection is configurable in future.

functional: Lower agent ID is proposer (comparing agent1.id < agent2.id lexicographically)
Docstring explains proposer selection rule and future configurability
theory: Protocol correctly applies TIOLI logic per O&R Ch 3

features.name: Implement AsymmetricNashBargainingProtocol class
features.description: Create AsymmetricNashBargainingProtocol that uses agent.bargaining_power to compute weights: β = w1/(w1+w2). Wraps existing asymmetric_nash_bargaining_solution().

property: When both agents have equal bargaining_power, result equals NashBargainingProtocol

features.name: Export new protocols from microecon package
features.description: Add TIOLIBargainingProtocol, AsymmetricNashBargainingProtocol, and tioli_bargaining_solution to microecon/__init__.py exports.
verification_commands: uv run python -c "from microecon import TIOLIBargainingProtocol, AsymmetricNashBargainingProtocol"

features.name: Theory tests: TIOLI proposer extracts full surplus
features.description: Test that TIOLI proposer receives maximum utility consistent with responder's IR constraint. Verify via grid search that no feasible allocation gives proposer more while responder >= disagreement.

theory: Proposer utility is maximal subject to responder IR (O&R Ch 3)

features.name: Theory tests: TIOLI responder at indifference
features.description: Test that TIOLI responder receives exactly their disagreement utility (within tolerance). This is the defining property of TIOLI.

theory: Responder indifferent between accepting and rejecting (O&R Ch 3)

features.name: Theory tests: TIOLI proposer identity matters
features.description: Test that swapping which agent proposes swaps who receives the surplus. This distinguishes TIOLI from symmetric protocols.

functional: When agent 1 proposes: agent 1 gets high utility, agent 2 at disagreement
When agent 2 proposes: agent 2 gets high utility, agent 1 at disagreement
theory: Proposer advantage is complete in TIOLI (O&R Ch 3)

features.name: Theory tests: Asymmetric Nash protocol properties
features.description: Test AsymmetricNashBargainingProtocol: reduces to symmetric Nash when powers equal, higher power gives higher utility, maximizes weighted product.

functional: Tests use Agent objects with bargaining_power attribute (not raw function)
property: Equal bargaining_power → same result as NashBargainingProtocol

features.name: Server: Add protocol routing for new protocols
features.description: Update server/simulation_manager.py to handle 'tioli' and 'asymmetric_nash' protocol strings, instantiating the correct protocol classes.

functional: Default bargaining_power distribution when asymmetric_nash selected

verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_server.py -v -k protocol || echo 'Server tests may not exist yet'

features.name: UI: Update protocol selector with all four protocols
features.description: Update ConfigModal.tsx to show four protocol options: Nash (Symmetric), Nash (Patience), Nash (Power), Take-It-Or-Leave-It.

functional: Correct protocol string sent to server

verification_commands: Manual: Open config modal, verify 4 protocol options

features.name: UI: Add tooltips explaining each protocol
features.description: Add hover tooltips to each protocol option explaining what it does and when to use it. Use the naming convention from ADR.

functional: Nash (Symmetric): 'Equal split of gains. Neither agent has bargaining advantage.'
Nash (Patience): 'Patient agents (high δ) get larger share. Based on Rubinstein/BRW.'
Nash (Power): 'Agents with higher bargaining_power get larger share.'
TIOLI: 'Proposer extracts all gains. Responder gets exactly their outside option.'

verification_commands: Manual: Hover over each protocol, verify tooltip text

features.name: UI: Add bargaining_power distribution config
features.description: When Nash (Power) / Asymmetric Nash is selected, show distribution selector for bargaining_power: Uniform (default), Gaussian, Bimodal. Pass to server.

functional: Distribution selector appears when asymmetric_nash protocol selected
Options: Uniform [0.5, 1.5], Gaussian (μ=1, σ=0.3), Bimodal (0.5 and 1.5)

verification_commands: Manual: Select Nash (Power), verify distribution dropdown appears

features.name: Server: Handle bargaining_power distribution
features.description: Update create_simulation() to distribute bargaining_power across agents according to selected distribution when asymmetric_nash protocol is used.

functional: Agents created with appropriate bargaining_power values

verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_simulation.py -v -k bargaining_power || echo 'Test may need creation'

features.name: Update CLAUDE.md with new protocols
features.description: Add TIOLI and Asymmetric Nash to the bargaining protocols section in CLAUDE.md. Explain the distinction between patience-weighted (Rubinstein) and power-weighted (Asymmetric Nash).

functional: All four protocols listed with brief descriptions
Clear explanation of power source differences

features.name: Update STATUS.md with new protocols
features.description: Update STATUS.md bargaining protocols table to include TIOLI and Asymmetric Nash with their theoretical references.

functional: Protocols table includes all four protocols

features.name: Ensure protocol docstrings explain differences
features.description: Review and update docstrings for all four BargainingProtocol implementations to clearly explain what distinguishes each and when to use it.

functional: Each protocol class has comprehensive docstring
Differences between similar protocols explicitly noted

verification_commands: uv run python -c "from microecon.bargaining import TIOLIBargainingProtocol; help(TIOLIBargainingProtocol)"

resolved.rationale: Groups Nash-family protocols by prefix while clarifying power source in parenthetical. TIOLI distinct enough to not need 'Nash' prefix.
Deterministic, simple, documented as configurable in future. Avoids random selection which complicates reproducibility.

resolved.choice: Closed-form solution, not asymmetric Nash with extreme β
resolved.rationale: Cleaner semantically (responder exactly at indifference), avoids numerical edge cases with β→1.

resolved.choice: Use new bargaining_power attribute, not discount_factor
resolved.rationale: Enables studying patience vs institutional power as orthogonal variables. Rubinstein already covers patience-derived power.

escalation_triggers: Theory test fails with divergence > 1e-3 (may indicate implementation bug or theory misunderstanding)
TIOLI responder utility significantly differs from disagreement point
Asymmetric Nash with equal powers differs from symmetric Nash by > 1e-6
Changes to existing bargaining.py break existing tests
